title: christella wailele
intro: Christella wailele, also known as the Maile, is a type of evergreen vine native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae and is a member of the fern family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Christella wailele include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared through herbal infusions, teas, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
